element_introduction_order.mastery_context: core tool for defeating enemies and breaking environmental obstacles
thematic antagonist encounters building toward final confrontation
climactic showdown requiring mastery of all previously learned mechanics

typical_tension_triggers: advancing toward Rusty Nail Inc. confrontation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating difficulty curve with building tension toward final boss confrontation; stage-based progression with recovery opportunities between encounters

difficulty_curve.early_game: introductory combat encounters with basic enemies; hammer mechanic establishment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: increased enemy density; introduction of powerup mechanics; corporate enemy types introduce thematic progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: sustained pressure from Rusty Nail Inc. forces; boss encounter preparation
notable_spikes: final boss encounter with Rusty Nail Inc. leadership

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential levels building narrative of carpenter Harry confronting and dismantling Rusty Nail Inc.'s operation; each stage represents geographic or corporate progression toward final confrontation
Beat-em-up paradigm emphasizes enemy encounters and wave-based combat rather than platforming puzzles; hammer as primary interaction tool
Regionally-differentiated powerups (sake in Japan, hot pepper in Europe; rice ball vs. cheese) introduced strategically during enemy encounters to sustain player health

sources_used: tcrf - Hammerin' Harry (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
